- KeyError: 'pr_id' - KeyError: 'pr_id' - KeyError: 'pr_id' All 11 failures are **KeyError: 'pr_id'** — tests are trying to access a dictionary key `'pr_id'` that doesn't exist. This suggests a recent field rename from `pr_id` to something else (or vice versa) in the workflow inputs/metadata that tests haven't been updated to reflect. Given the branch name `plnd/O9109-update-test-plan-to-03-10-2146`, this appears to be an ongoing refactoring related to field renaming.

diff --git a/tests/fakes/gateway/github.py b/tests/fakes/gateway/github.py index 784caae7c..427cd48d9 100644 --- a/tests/fakes/gateway/github.py +++ b/tests/fakes/gateway/github.py @@ -94,7 +94,7 @@ class FakeLocalGitHub(LocalGitHub): workflow_runs_by_node_id: Mapping of GraphQL node_id -> WorkflowRun for get_workflow_runs_by_node_ids() run_logs: Mapping of run_id -> log string - pr_plan_linkages: Mapping of plan_number -> list[PullRequestInfo] + pr_plan_linkages: Mapping of pr_number -> list[PullRequestInfo] polled_run_id: Run ID to return from poll_for_workflow_run (None for timeout) authenticated: Whether gh CLI is authenticated (default True for test convenience) auth_username: Username returned by check_auth_status() (default "test-user") @@ -480,20 +480,20 @@ class FakeLocalGitHub(LocalGitHub): def get_prs_linked_to_issues( self, location: GitHubRepoLocation, - plan_numbers: list[int], + pr_numbers: list[int], ) -> dict[int, list[PullRequestInfo]]: """Get PRs linked to plans (returns pre-configured data).
- Returns only the mappings for plans in plan_numbers that have + Returns only the mappings for plans in pr_numbers that have pre-configured PR linkages. Plans without linkages are omitted.
The location parameter is accepted but ignored - fake returns pre-configured data regardless of the location. """ result = {}

def test_prepare_plan_converts_identifier_to_int() -> None: """Plan identifier string is converted to pr number int.""" plan = _make_plan( pr_identifier="789", header_fields={"branch_name": "plan-test-01-01-0000"}, ) timestamp = datetime(2024, 1, 1, 0, 0)
result = prepare_plan_for_worktree(plan, timestamp, warn_non_open=True)

_____________________ test_happy_path_with_existing_issue ______________________
def test_happy_path_with_existing_issue() -> None: """Command succeeds with --pr flag, using existing plan number.""" runner = CliRunner() with erk_isolated_fs_env(runner, env_overrides=None) as env: fake_github = FakeLocalGitHub() fake_issues = FakeGitHubIssues() ctx = env.build_context( current_branch="my-feature", github=fake_github, issues=fake_issues, )
result = runner.invoke( cli, ["admin", "test-plan-implement-gh-workflow", "--pr", "42"], obj=ctx )
assert result.exit_code == 0, f"Command failed: {result.output}" # Verify plan 42 was used assert "Using existing PR #42" in result.output # Verify PR was created as draft assert len(fake_github.created_prs) == 1 branch, title, _body, base, draft = fake_github.created_prs[0] assert branch.startswith("test-workflow-") assert base == "main" assert draft is True # Verify workflow was triggered assert len(fake_github.triggered_workflows) == 1 workflow, inputs, _ref = fake_github.triggered_workflows[0]

88→ assert plan_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8") == plan_body 89→ 90→ ref_file = impl_context_dir / "ref.json" 91→ assert ref_file.exists() 92→ ref_data = json.loads(ref_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8")) 93→ assert ref_data["provider"] == "github-draft-pr" 94→ assert ref_data["pr_id"] == "123" 95→ assert ref_data["url"] == "https://github.com/test-owner/test-repo/pull/123" 96→ assert "created_at" in ref_data 97→ assert "synced_at" in ref_data 98→ 99→

370→ 371→ # Output JSON has correct metadata 372→ output_lines = result.output.strip().split("\n") 373→ json_line = next(line for line in reversed(output_lines) if line.startswith("{")) 374→ output = json.loads(json_line) 375→ assert output["success"] is True 376→ assert output["title"] == "Local Plan" 377→ 378→ 379→def test_planned_pr_reads_objective_id_from_ref_json(tmp_path: Path) -> None:
